title: Phoebus Olog Shift Module
description: Java plugin for the Phoebus control system that automatically attaches active shift metadata to electronic logbook entries via REST API. Used at NSLS-II at Brookhaven National Laboratory.

title: DMWSS — Game Boy Emulator
description: High-performance, modular Game Boy emulator in modern C++20. Features a cached instruction interpreter, software fastmem optimization, event-driven scheduling, and a mode-accurate PPU renderer with OpenGL support.

title: Lectura
description: AI-powered lecture notes tool built in 36 hours at SBUHacks 2025. Converts audio recordings into summaries, flashcards, and translations across 50+ languages. Won MLH Best Use of Gemini API.

title: CHIP-8 Educational Emulator
description: Educational CHIP-8 emulator that visualizes internal CPU state, registers, memory, and the stack in real time as programs execute. Built to teach how emulators work from first principles.

title: MyrientDL
description: Polite, resumable async web crawler and download manager for the Myrient game preservation archive. Features fuzzy search, SQLite tracking, and rate-limited downloads. Now defunct following the Myrient project shutdown.

title: Phoebus Queue Server Application
description: JavaFX client integrating Bluesky Queue Server into the Phoebus control system, enabling real-time plan management for beamline scientists at NSLS-II without leaving the Phoebus environment. Achieves 100% request success rate with 18.6ms mean response time.

title: OpenGL Portal Demo
description: Real-time recursive portal rendering using OpenGL stencil buffers. Portals correctly display the view from the opposite side with proper depth handling and nested recursion.

title: Intel 8080 Space Invaders Emulator
description: Cycle-accurate Intel 8080 CPU emulator running the original Space Invaders (1978) arcade game, complete with hardware-accurate timing, interrupt handling, and the original cabinet's dual-screen color overlay.

title: termplayer
description: Full-featured ASCII video player for the terminal. Converts video frames to ASCII art and plays them with synchronized audio using FFmpeg. Famous for playing Bad Apple in a terminal.
